entries.rationale: The flat skill layout remains true, but this record is a composite that also asserts the obsolete ai: namespace. Current runtime surfaces standardize ai-, and DEC-021 is the cleaner naming decision, so DEC-001 no longer reads as a reliable active record.

Each entry has a later owner or replacement surface in the current repository: later agent redesigns, DEC-028 observability, DEC-029 artifact-driven release flow, DEC-030 conventional commits, and DEC-039 orchestrator dispatch. These are useful historical records, not active policy.
notes: Grouped because all members are already displaced by a clearly newer canonical surface.

entries.rationale: The human gate principle is still current: AGENTS.md still requires a spec-approved workflow before implementation. The stored command name is stale, though; current execution goes through /ai-dispatch rather than /ai-execute.
notes: Normalize the command reference to the current approved execution surface.

entries.rationale: These governance decisions still match the current repository shape: flat feature-branch workflow, concise stack-context guidance, and root overlays that delegate to shared canonical governance files instead of duplicating them wholesale.

This entry is written as a future implementation target for a GOVERNANCE_SOURCE.md surface that does not exist. Current single-source governance is carried by the Constitution, AGENTS.md, manifest metadata, and generated mirrors, so DEC-005 reads like abandoned roadmap text rather than an audit-grade active decision.
notes: Move the remaining intent to spec or backlog history if it still matters.

entries.rationale: These tooling and platform choices remain visible in current code and CI: SonarCloud is still a primary gate, Snyk remains optional via SNYK_TOKEN, provider detection still supports GitHub and Azure DevOps, and staged gitleaks remains the local secret-scan path.

The operative no-suppression rule now lives in the Constitution rather than in this standalone DEC. The historical record is still useful, but its linkage is incomplete because the current canonical home is outside the entry metadata.
notes: Point the historical record at the constitutional replacement or explicit successor record.

entries.rationale: This entry references checkpoint.execute and checkpoint.release for execute and release agents that are absent from the current agent roster and not corroborated by nearby code or current root governance files. It has archaeology value, but not active governance value.

The repository still has 10 agents, but the current roster in manifest.yml and AGENTS.md differs materially from the execute, ship, observe, write, and operate roster described here. The existing superseded_by link points to DEC-028 observability rather than the architecture that actually displaced this roster.
notes: Current roster: plan, build, verify, guard, review, explore, guide, simplify, autopilot, run-orchestrator.

entries.rationale: These runtime governance and orchestration decisions still match current files: dependabot exemptions are encoded in CI, ai- hyphen syntax is canonical, autopilot remains the explicit autonomous override, Copilot orchestration is injected through mirror sync, and observability centers on framework-events plus agentsview.

This record claims the gh-aw migration is complete, but the repository surface does not support that claim: .github/aw contains only an actions lock file, and solution-intent still marks runbook automation as in progress. That mismatch makes DEC-022 unreliable as an active decision record.
notes: If the migration is still desired, track it in spec/backlog state instead of the active decision store.

entries.rationale: These are already-closed outcomes rather than live governance rules: the pygments CVE was remediated and the contexts/orgs cleanup is already realized in the current tree shape. They should remain discoverable as history, not occupy the active decision surface.

The contract cleanup itself is real, but the replacement mapping is stale: current documentation says Constitution-driven governance replaced project-identity, and no project-identity artifact or /ai-project-identity surface is present in the live repo. Treat this as completed cleanup history with broken linkage.
notes: Normalize the replacement mapping to the Constitution plus current root entry-point surfaces.

entries.rationale: Current release and build workflows align with these delivery decisions: artifact-driven releases, conventional commits, CI Check and CI Build decomposition, attestation and SBOM generation, a single version source rooted in pyproject.toml, and 90-day workflow artifact retention with release attachment for permanence.
This accepted pip risk is still active and unexpired, and the current pip-audit integration plus recent spec history still treat the CVE as known accepted debt rather than resolved debt.

This risk is still active and referenced in the current pip-audit allow-list, but the decision text describes a transient upgrade window that should be re-evaluated before the 2026-05-26 expiry. It is live risk now, but it is the closest candidate for closure or renewal review.

The registry still carries multiple sha256_pinned=False TODOs tied directly to DEC-038, and the runtime mechanism still preserves the unpinned bypass path as an explicit mitigation. This remains a live governance risk, not completed cleanup.

These spec-104 gate decisions are actively reflected in current policy code and context docs: orchestrator-based dispatch, the local fast-slice versus CI-authoritative split, and gate-cache semantics all exist as live implementation, not just prose.

highlights.issue: Stored namespace language conflicts with the current ai- prefix and overlaps with DEC-021.
The stored workflow still names /ai-execute, but the current approved execution surface is /ai-dispatch.
GOVERNANCE_SOURCE.md is referenced as the target SSOT, but that surface does not exist in the current repo model.
checkpoint.execute and checkpoint.release reference removed execute and release agent concepts.
The entry is marked active, but its agent roster no longer matches the current 10-agent set and its superseded_by points at unrelated observability work.
The entry claims gh-aw migration is complete, but the current gh-aw directory contains only actions-lock.json and solution-intent still marks the work in progress.
The replacement mapping still points at project-identity even though current documentation says Constitution-driven governance replaced that surface.

invariants.rule: Every entry classified as live risk must correspond to a risk-acceptance record with status=active and a non-null future expires_at.
No expired risk-acceptance may remain in the live-risk slice after normalization; expired active risks must fail the invariant and require review or reclassification.
Every superseded-history record must have either a valid successor linkage or an explicit archived-home explanation; broken superseded_by pointers must fail.
A formal decision may not reference removed commands, removed agents, or nonexistent canonical files once normalization is complete.
Completed-cleanup records must carry a terminal lifecycle state and must be excluded from the active-decision slice used for current governance posture.

Every retained formal decision and live risk must have normalized lineage: non-empty spec/source metadata, no placeholder RECONSTRUCTED marker unless the entry is explicitly archived, and a source that matches current repo history.

After cleanup, the active decision store surface must contain only formal decisions and live risks; superseded, completed, and archive-candidate material must be explicitly partitioned out of the active slice.

Any entry that references a missing canonical artifact such as GOVERNANCE_SOURCE.md, project-identity, removed agent names, or /ai-execute must fail normalization until it is archived, reclassified, or repaired.
